what_it_does: Records a channel-memory attachment that points to a board item via a board:// URI, with type and timestamp metadata.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to reference an external board item.
It is a minimal, metadata-only attachment record.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Uses a URI-style reference for cross-resource linking.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry linking a dragnet-development memory item to board item item-a5b7cf79, with no explanatory body.
questions: What content or context does this attachment carry beyond the link?
What is the referenced board item item-a5b7cf79?
Why is there no body or summary text?
purpose_vs_actual: The file does exactly what its metadata suggests: it records a typed, timestamped link to a board item.
intention_signals: To create a durable, retrievable association between a channel memory attachment and a specific board item for later navigation or context reconstruction.
novelty: Use of a board:// URI as the target value for an attachment reference.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed edge between a channel memory attachment and a board item, enabling graph traversal and provenance tracking without adding content.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record linking dragnet-development channel memory attachment att-a5360231 to board item item-a5b7cf79 as board_items.
intention_for_embedding: To record a typed, timestamped association between a channel memory attachment and a board item.
